reason:
CLOSE-OUT REASON (Set 089, session 1)

Operator-authorized close of a code-only evidence-completeness set past a
residual blocking verdict whose two findings the operator adjudicated as
verifier CATEGORY ERRORS, not defects. Cross-provider verification ran normally
via the API path across two rounds; round 1 found two REAL bugs (fixed), round 2
CONFIRMS the fixes and raised only the two category errors. Verdict WAIVED.

MANUAL-VERIFY ATTESTATION

- Verifying surface: ai_router `verify_session` (routed API), rounds 1 and 2.
- Verifier model: gpt-5-4; effective provider: openai -- DISTINCT from the
  orchestrator's effective provider (anthropic / claude-opus-4-8), i.e. the
  cross-provider exclusion the automated path enforces was satisfied.
- Template: the canonical adversarial `session-verification` template (standard
  verify_session route; not a hand-rolled review).
- Timestamp: 2026-07-10 (rounds 1 and 2 run this date).
- Raw verification artifacts: docs/session-sets/089-verify-evidence-completeness/
  s1-verification.md and s1-verification-round-2.md (with s1-issues.json /
  s1-issues-round-2.json).

SUBSTANCE (why this is a confident close, not a rubber stamp)

The loop worked correctly on the substance. Round 1 raised two genuine Major
findings that cited this session's own spec:
 1. the depth-agnostic dist exclude dropped a nested SOURCE dir (src/dist) --
    a completeness regression -- FIXED by adding explicit tracked-excluded
    reporting (EvidenceBundle.tracked_excluded + rendered section);
 2. Fix B's oversized guard lived only in run(), not threaded through
    assemble_evidence -- FIXED by raising EvidenceTooLargeError from
    assemble_evidence so every caller fails closed.
Round 2 (same different-provider verifier) EXPLICITLY CONFIRMS both fixes landed
and raised NO defect against the delivered code. Full suite green: 2913 passed.

ADJUDICATION (why WAIVED, not remediated further)

The two residual round-2 findings are non-defects:
 A. "round-1 artifact is stale/false" -- round-1 s1-verification.md is an
    immutable, append-only point-in-time record; round 2 correctly appended
    sibling artifacts after the two real gaps were fixed. Editing the round-1
    artifact would itself violate the raw-record rule.
 B. "the set is not closed yet" (no change-log/disposition/close at verify time)
    -- unsatisfiable at Step 6, which necessarily precedes close-out (Step 8+).
    Raised round 1 (finding 3), resurrected round 2 -- the same circular
    category error seen closing Set 088.

Full adjudication: docs/session-sets/089-verify-evidence-completeness/
s1-adjudication.md. The operator accepted the adjudication (interactive
selection, 2026-07-10) and authorized this operator-attested close. A follow-up
to teach the verifier that pre-close lifecycle state is not a defect is
recommended (the ss4 doc's deferred prompt-template item) so this override stops
recurring.
